front: Work: D'Alembert's Preliminary Discourse introduces this project with a chart dividing knowledge into Memory, Reason, and Imagination
Work: Louis de Jaucourt contributed 17,266 articles averaging eight per day to this reference work, and passionately denounced slavery
Work: compares a synchronized hive of bees to a multi-headed animal while exploring how living beings emerge from networks of cells
Philosopher: imprisoned after revealing atheism in the 1749 Letter on the Blind; argued the eye can learn and adjust after surgery in response to Molyneux's problem
Philosopher: introduced the concept of the 'fourth wall' and wrote Jacques the Fatalist and Rameau's Nephew
Work: a Tahitian named Orou discusses the 'all-knowing craftsman' with a chaplain who sleeps with Orou's daughter
